pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 SCHEMES > TIDE 2.0 Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs (TIDE 2.0) TIDE 2.0 will promote tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups primarily engaged in using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The Scheme will be implemented through empowering 51 incubators in India and handholding close to 2000 tech start-ups over a period of 5 years. The scheme envisions creating a holistic ecosystem to support technology startups and incubation centers through identifying and creating necessary linkages. MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) would be setup under TIDE 2.0 to ensure synergistic linkages among the TIDE 2.0 centres, theme based incubation centres, Centre of Excellences on emerging technologies and other collaborative platforms. The MSH will also ensure crisscrossing of technology resources across the innovation ecosystem to come out with technology solutions to various societal challenges. Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs (TIDE 2.0) Scheme has been envisaged to promote tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in seven pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The Scheme will be implemented through 51 incubators and handholding of approximately 2000 tech start-ups over a period of five years. It will support tech startups addressing societal challenges in seven selected thematic areas identified based on national priorities in the realms of: Success Stories of TIDE 2.0 Supported Startups Salient Features of the Scheme: TIDE 2.0 Centers: Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organizations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters so as to provide necessary mentorship and possible infusion of innovative businesses into their larger business operations. To leverage the diversity in the ecosystem, it is proposed to roll out TIDE 2.0 in a layered approach. For this it is envisaged that each of the 51 TIDE 2.0 Centres will be categorized into three distinct groups as summarized below: Group 1 Centers View Group 2 Centers View Group 3 Centers View List of TIDE 2.0 Supported Startups MSH Incubation Center Statistics: TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centre List of TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centers Important Links: Administrative Approval List of Incubation Centres under TIDE 2.0

Hence, innovation and entrepreneurship is the emerging focus area that is being aggressively promoted to give fillip to the Indian economy. Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India is leading and facilitating a gamut of Innovation and IPR-related activities across the country towards the expansion of this ecosystem. READ MORE MSH OFFERINGS As MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) sees consolidation of its activities bringing on board a bevy of startups, incubators, mentors and COEs in its quest to emerge as a single biggest collaborative platform for tech startup community, setting up innovative partnerships and forging meaningful synergies with all stakeholders of the tech ecosystem in the country has been its primary focus. This technology integrates Optical MSI and SAR sensors to deliver intuitive data, crucial for sectors like defence, surveillance, agriculture, insurance, and aquaculture. GalaxEye claims to have also developed India’s First UAV SAR system for defence markets and completed over 300 successful flights with UAV SAR Payload. Samaaro connects with your CRM, marketing automation platform, and more, streamlining your workflow and centralizing event data. Eliminate silos, automate tasks, and unlock the full potential of your event ecosystem. Read story India Accelerator’s iAngels network invests in Fruitfal Fruitfal is a full-stack agri platform-enabling more efficient demand and supply discovery in the verified agri value chain. Fruitfal’s full agri stack makes this process of discovery of demand and supply more efficient connecting qualified buyers with certified suppliers via a mobile-native cloud platform. Our unique algorithms match demand to supply and provide reliable market linkages for producers both domestically and internationally. Read story Freestand selected among Top 20 startups at cohort of Google for Startups Accelerator, India Platform that enables FMCG brands to distribute product samples across both online and offline platforms. Our platform simplifies the planning, auditing and measurement processes involved in executing product sampling campaigns. Brief of the product: Omnichannel Platform for FMCG product sampling (Cloud hosted 2 sided B2B Platform). Two sides of the platform include: -Supply Side: Sampling vendors(E-commerce, Q-Commerce, Physical Destinations) -Demand Side: FMCG Brand and Media Agencies Read story Tan90 raises Rs 11.32 crore in pre-series A round Tan90 develops and manufactures passive coolants called Phase Change Materials (PCM) for thermal management, maintaining temperatures between +50 to -24 degrees Celsius across various applications, from cold chain to building cooling.Our portable cold storage solution has a 58-liter capacity and uses proprietary thermal batteries that charge twice as fast as market alternatives, providing quicker turnaround times. These self-standing cooling boxes can be stacked and transported using any logistics service, eliminating the need for refrigerated trucks and reducing operational costs by 32%.To extend the shelf life of perishables, Tan90 introduces hydantoin-based antimicrobial polymers, enhancing longevity without cold storage. Read story Corefactors: Revolutionizing RevOps with AI-driven CRM, Wins "Tech StartUp of the Year" Award 2023. Corefactors was founded in 2015 with the vision of building a product and solving customer problems in the sales and marketing space. Initially, we started to understand multiple businesses’ sales processes and operations while catering for them with traditional marketing solutions like SMS marketing & Missed Call Services. They provide a RevOps-enabling AI-driven Automated Relationship Management solution, a CRM which automates the sales process, cuts costs, enables revops and reduces revenue leakages to ZERO. Read story DroneTech Startup TSAW launches its logistics service division DRONECO TSAW is a drone tech company that is pioneering the future of advanced air mobility with innovative hardware and software solutions. We are committed to developing and deploying safe, efficient, and sustainable drone solutions for a variety of industries, including logistics, agriculture, and surveillance. Our team of experienced engineers is at the forefront of hardware and software development for advanced air mobility. TSAW is building a network of drone hubs (inhouse designed structures to facilitate landing and take-off of any drone) and corridors to make drones as a go-to mode of transportation for cargo movements. They are developing routes of our Network at various strategic locations for multiple clients, and are operating drones on these routes. The prime focus markets for TSAW are Medical Logistics, E-Commerce and Quick-Commerce. They are India’s First Smart Luggage Brand having launched Patented Smart Wallet, Smart Bags, Smart follow-me Luggage. The products are Fingerprint lock bags, anti-lost wallets and bags, Smart accessories, Smart follow me luggage. Arista Vault, a pioneering technology firm with 8 patents and 6 trademarks, is dedicated to simplifying and enhancing human life through innovative smart products and gadgets. Automated swap stations for allowing users to swap the batteries without any manual interventions. The platform offers scalability, customization, AI-based predictive models, web & mobile application modules and can integrate with any third-party system. AI & DeepTech Pavilion Takes Center Stage at Startup Mahakumbh, Showcasing India’s Tech Prowess Aug 2025 Read More The AI & DeepTech Pavilion at Startup Mahakumbh kicked off today, bringing together over 100 startups, industry leaders, and policymakers to explore the latest advancements in artificial intelligence and deep technology. The first day of the event served as a platform for significant announcements, including MeitY’s AI-driven ‘MeitY Mitra’ chatbot launch, unveiling of a comprehensive Nasscom-Zinnov Tech Startup Report analyzing India’s AI and deep-tech landscape and power-packed panels featuring industry KOLs and experts. MSH Startup policy forum to collaborate to support startup ecosystem. MeitY Startup Hub Partners SPF To Foster Deeptech Innovation Aug 2025 Read More The partnership will focus on startups across sectors, including AI, deep technology and other emerging technologies SPF will assist MSH in several initiatives, including conducting joint research, policy analysis and organising stakeholder engagement As per DPIIT data till January 31, 2025, India is home to more than 1.61 Lakh startups MeitY Startup Hub throws open innovation challenge to build open-source consent systems for data protection law Aug 2025 Read More The winning solution will be made available by the MeitY as a downloadable public good. It would help standardise consent handling practices India’s digital ecosystem. Investor Connect Programme in Shillong Fosters Startup Growth Aug 2024 Read More The Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) and MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) have hosted an Investor Connect Programme in Shillong, Meghalaya, aimed at connecting technology startups with prominent investors and government stakeholders. The event featured seven esteemed investors, including Dr. Vibhuti Aggarwal of Realtime Angel Fund, Bhavish Sood of Modulor Capital, Abhishek Gulia of Agility Ventures, Gaurav Chauhan of Mankind Family Office, Aaquib Hussain of Freeflow Ventures, Pranta Pratim Singha of NRL Venture Fund, and Sadique Mannan from IIM Calcutta Innovation Park. Meet The 10 Startups That Made Ripples At MSH ‘Investor Connect Programme’ In Bhubaneswar May 2024 Read More The programme provided an opportunity to 10 startups from various sectors like deeptech, healthtech and agritech to showcase their offerings to investors Held at KIIT Technology Business Incubator (KIIT-TBI) on May 11, the event was a part of a nationwide series of such programmes being conducted by the MSH The startups that participated in the event were from the portfolios of KIIT-TBI, FTBI, and STPI MSH Investor Connect Programme Reaches Chennai To Unlock Funding Opportunities For Tamil Nadu Startups May 2024 Read More The MeitY Startup Hub’s ‘Investor Connect Programme’ will be held at Forge Fast Accelerator at IIT Madras Research Park in Chennai on May 3, 2024. Over 10 industrial deeptech startups will pitch to 30+ investors, including Apurva Chamaria from Google India, Naveena Reddy from Lead Angels Fund, Vignesh Shekhar from Artha99.VC at the event. FabHeads Automation, AugurAI, PQSI Digital, Suzhiyam and Deliverain are among the startups which will showcase their products and services at the event. Meet The 10 Startups That Gained Spotlight At The Mumbai Edition Of MSH Investor Connect Programme May 2024 Read More The MSH Investor Connect Programme in Mumbai attracted over 20 investors, including Rishiraj Kalita (AVP, Huddle Ventures), Rhea Sthalekar (VP, Capria Ventures), Kanchi Daiya (regional head – west India, IAN) and representatives from 100x.VC, Ah! Ventures, Ankur Capital, Vaillant Capital Partners, Blume Ventures, Equanimity Ventures and Piper Serica. The programme featured a wide range of startups from sectors such as healthtech, deeptech and electric vehicles. The event fostered valuable discussions between investors and government stakeholders on strengthening collaboration and empowering early stage startups. MeitY Startup Hub Investor Connect Programme Brings Together 10 Startups, 25+ Investors In Delhi NCR Apr 2024 Read More MeitY Startup Hub Investor Connect Programme was held at Amity Innovation Incubator in Delhi NCR on April 10, 2024. Ten startups solving unique real-life problems pitched to over 20 investors at the event. The programme, organised by MSH and supported by Amity Innovation Incubator and Inc42, saw startups from sectors like fintech, edtech, AI, analytics, and agritech showcase their products and solutions to investors. VIEW MORE NEWS MeitY Startup Hub: Empowering innovation, driving growth and forging success within the vast landscape of Startup Mahakhumbh Startup Mahakhumb 2024 marks the commencement of an unprecedented event uniting India's dynamic startup ecosystem under one roof Hosted at Bharat Mandapam in New Delhi from March 18-20, 2024. With the resounding theme of 'Bharat Innovates,' aims to catalyze innovation, facilitate networking, and foster growth opportunities for startups, investors, incubators, accelerators and industry leaders across diverse sectors. One of the flagship engagement of the event is MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) innovation showcase, MeitY Pavilion emerged as a hub of innovation and entrepreneurship today as over 40+ ground-breaking startups seized the opportunity to showcase their cutting-edge innovations and served as a dynamic platform for startups to engage with industry experts, investors and potential partners, fostering valuable connections and opportunities for growth. Attendees had the chance to explore firsthand the creativity, ingenuity, and potential of these emerging startups, further enriching the collaborative spirit of the event. Addressing critical challenges and opportunities within the startup space. Shri Jeej Vijay, CEO of MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), brought invaluable insights to one such panel discussion, focusing on "Funding DeepTech: Venture Capital's Perspective on Research-Backed Startups." The program attracted the interest of many startup and investment giants , including BIORX, RTAF, Sanchi Connect, IvyCamp, Lawsikho, Skill Arbitrage, HPCL, Cummins India, India Angel Network, SIDBI, Singhania & Co., Cummins India, Danazir Wealth Management, etc. [ May 3, 2024 ] Read More MeitY Startup Hub Investor Connect Programme’s Jaipur Edition Spotlights 10 Startups Meity Startup Hub (MSH) held the Investor Connect Programme in Jaipur on March 9. The programme hosted investors such as Sanat Mondal, VP and principal at IAN Alpha Fund; Rishiraj Kalita, AVP at Huddle Ventures; Dr. Shweta Choudhary of Innovher and more. The presenting 10 startups came from various MeitY-backed programmes like SAMRIDH, TIDE 2.0, and centres of excellence. At Startup Mahakumbh, the MSH will host several workshops and masterclasses with industry veterans and unicorn founders. The discussions will delve into topics like crafting a business model canvas and pitching and funding strategies to accelerate the growth of startups. Powered by Inc42 and supported by IIM Ahmedabad’s entrepreneurship centre, IIMA Ventures, the programme will also help MeitY’s incubation centres build robust investor networks. Earlier, at the Investor Connect Programme held in Jaipur, over 10 promising startups pitched their ideas to a panel of investors. Around 40 high-performing startups from MeitY's flagship initiatives such as SAMRIDH and TIDE 2.0 also received the opportunity to showcase their innovations. pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 MeitY Startup Hub India is among top 3 startup ecosystems in the world. In order to boost the Indian economy, innovation and entrepreneurship in the emerging focus area that is being aggressively promoted to give fillip to the Indian economy. A variety of innovation and IPR-related activities are being led and facilitated nationwide by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, in an effort to grow this ecosystem. In order to facilitate MeitY’s vision of promoting technology innovation, start-ups and creation of Intellectual Properties, a nodal entity called ‘MeitY Start-up Hub’ (MSH) has been setup under its aegis. MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), is a unique, high-impact, digitally-driven scheme which seeks to strengthen the startup ecosystem by integrating the resources, upskilling the entrepreneurs, leveraging the experts, facilitating the knowledge transactions, and promoting the investments in deep-tech startups to support inclusive & sustainable techno-socio-economic development across India and international expansion programs for startups among other activities. To support and leverage the startup ecosystem we host Startups, Incubators, Centres of Entrepreneurship (CoEs), Accelerators, Mentors and Investor. MSH is actively engaged in conducting different Programs, Challenges and Hackathons, both supported and hosted in conjunction with Government bodies, Ministries, Corporates etc. in addition to capacity building workshops across a plethora of topics of interest to our beneficiaries, hosted on our one stop digital platform on Youtube called "MSH Startup School."

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > SBI MeitY Startup Hub Partners with State Bank of India Enabling Startup Growth Through Strategic Financial Access As part of its mission to build a robust and inclusive startup ecosystem, MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has partnered with the State Bank of India (SBI) — India’s largest public sector bank — to empower startups with the right mix of financial access, strategic mentorship, and ecosystem enablement. Building Awareness: Beyond Banking SBI joins hands with MSH not just as a banking institution, but as a committed growth partner. Through this collaboration, startups will benefit from: Access to financial literacy programs and mentorship Exposure to market insights and capital-readiness support Guidance on financial planning, compliance, and risk management This awareness-focused approach ensures that founders make informed, confident financial decisions as they scale. Enhancing Accessibility: Financing What’s Next With this partnership, startups can tap into SBI’s startup-centric offerings, including: Equity-linked funding opportunities Tailored credit lines and working capital support Support through SBI’s pan-India branch and digital network A Step Toward Inclusive Startup Growth By bridging the gap between innovation and financial infrastructure, the MSH x SBI partnership is set to power a new wave of entrepreneurship, from metropolitan hubs to Tier II/III cities.Together, we aim to create an enabling environment where startups are not only funded, but also mentored, connected, and equipped for sustainable growth. Are you a startup looking to scale with the right financial partner? Explore tailored offerings and startup-focused solutions from SBI here: SBI Startup Portal . This is your gateway to growth Get in Touch Looking to explore SBI’s startup offerings or need personalized assistance? Connect with dedicated SBI Startup Branches, listed below to get the right guidance and support tailored to your startup’s needs. Specialised Startup Branch, Hyderabad Email: Sbi.64338@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Chennai Email: Sbi.64320@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Bengaluru Email: Sbi.64074@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Pune Email: Sbi.64614@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Mumbai Email: Sbi.64369@sbi.co.in Specialised Startup Branch, Gurugram Email: Sbi.61808@sbi.co.in

Taking the shared vision forward The partnership aims to focus on startups particularly in the Electronics and IT sector. Deep-Tech Promote innovation and deep-tech solutions for startups. Networking Provide visibility, network access, and promotion support. Benefits Exclusive offers on Tally’s products/services for registered startups. Exclusive offer on Tally's products for registered startups Super charge your business with a FREE ONE-YEAR RENTAL LICENSE OF TALLYPRIME Get it Now ➝ About Tally Solutions Tally Solutions Pvt Ltd has been a pioneer in business software solutions since 1986. Its flagship product, TallyPrime, improves accounting, inventory, compliance, and business reporting efficiency. Serving over 2 million businesses and 7 million users across India and 100+ countries. Tally is committed to the Aatmanirbhar Bharat (A self-reliant India) initiative by enabling easier access to financial solutions for Indian businesses. With initiatives like the MeitY partnership and strategic partnerships with global tech players, Tally empowers MSMEs and contributes to a robust business ecosystem.

pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > RAZORPAY PARTNERSHIP Advancing India’s Startup Ecosystem: MeitY Startup Hub & Razorpay Partnership MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) is thrilled to announce a transformative strategic partnership with Razorpay , a collaboration poised to revolutionize the disruptors forming the Indian startup ecosystem. This strategic collaboration is meticulously designed to give early stage startups the boost they need to scale faster and smarter. WATCH LAUNCH VIDEO Proposed Benefits offered to all start-ups affiliated with MSH Programs: Turbocharge your operations with access to exclusive credits on Razorpay products: Exclusive access to MSH startups for credits on Razorpay’s 360 degree digital payments suite including Payment Gateway, Business Banking, and Payroll Exclusive knowledge sessions on topics: All the Startups associated with MSH, shall get exclusive access to knowledge sessions on various pertinent topics such as investor-ready pitch decks, referral design masterclass, content mastery etc.and meticulously curated best practices providing them with the strategic insights needed to navigate their entrepreneurial journey. Access to Razorpay Rize’s startup enablement program: Including access to YC resources, application review & mock interview preparations & pitch deck reviews by Razorpay Rize’s experts. Company incorporation: Access to exclusively discounted pricing and streamlined company registration services in India, with priority support to startups from MSH. Razorpay Rize Community: Selected startups to receive exclusive access to Razorpay Rize community which includes access to partner credits and deals. An extraordinary opportunity awaits! Sign up now to unlock exclusive benefits and resources through this groundbreaking collaboration. APPLY NOW WATCH FIRESIDE CHAT VIDEO

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MATHWORKS MeitY Startup Hub and MathWorks Partner to Accelerate Deep Tech Innovation MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) has signed a Statement of Intent (SoI) with MathWorks to bolster India’s deep tech startup ecosystem, providing access to state-of-the-art computational tools, advanced simulation models, and world-class training resources. As part of this partnership, MathWorks will provide complimentary access to over 100 products under its Accelerator Program , including MATLAB® and Simulink , for 12 months to all MeitY associated startups. This invaluable resource will empower startups to develop and refine their innovations with powerful computational capabilities. Beyond software access, participating startups will benefit from Technical Mentorship: Expert guidance to navigate complex technical challenges. Specialized Training: Tailored programs to maximize the utilization of MathWorks tools. Discounted Professional Access: Continued access to MathWorks products at preferential rates post-program. To further enhance industry engagement, MSH and MathWorks will conduct collaborative physical workshops at leading incubation centres across India. These workshops will empower startups, researchers, and industry professionals with the practical skills needed to address real-world challenges and accelerate innovation. Furthermore, MathWorks will offer advanced training at discounted fees, supporting faster innovation cycles and market access. Startups will also benefit from participation in MathWorks-organized events, such as MATLAB EXPO and webinars, along with opportunities to showcase their solutions on global platforms. We are thrilled to partner with MeitY Startup Hub to empower deep tech startups driving innovation in electrification, automation, and AI,” said Mr. Sunil Motwani, Country Manager, MathWorks India. “Through our tools and expertise, we aim to accelerate the development of transformative technologies that will shape the future of mobility and beyond. At MSH, we are excited to announce our commitment to empowering startups with advanced MathWorks products,” said Mr. Pannerselvam, CEO, MSH. “This partnership will support innovations that will provide startups with a significant edge in key technologies such as Artificial Intelligence, Wireless Communication, and Automation. This strategic deep tech partnership bridges the gap between technology and entrepreneurship, providing Indian startups with the essential resources to innovate, scale, and tackle global challenges. Together, MSH and MathWorks are building a thriving, future-ready startup ecosystem in India. DETAILS OF THE PROGRAM

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > MICRON MeitY Startup Hub (MSH), Micron and PIEDS, CREST (BITS Pilani), launch a Semiconductor Accelerator for Early-Stage Startups MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) an independent division of the Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in partnership with Micron and PIEDS, the Technology Business Incubator and Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) of BITS Pilani announced today the launch of CREST accelerator program for startups in Semiconductor domain. The accelerator program will curate a cohort of startups and provide each with funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs, with an opportunity to raise further investment at the end of the program. Mentors and advisors from Micron, MSH, and BITS Pilani shall guide the startups. The accelerator is being launched on the heels of the government’s big push to make India a semiconductor hub and an ambitious India Semiconductor Mission (ISM) by MeitY. The startups shall be able to use tools, labs, and facilities at Micron facilities and at the Centre for Research and Entrepreneurship in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST), BITS Pilani, Hyderabad campus, and the network of MeitY supported labs and facilities across India. Shri Rajnish Kumar, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, said, “We congratulate the PIDES team for their initiative to support semiconductor startups with funding, mentorship, and access to Micron’s facilities for product development. Together with Micron, we are committed to nurturing the next generation of pioneers in semiconductor technology” Sachin Arya, Head, PIEDS said, “The startups will play an integral role in India’s ambitious Semiconductor Mission, and further strengthen India’s indigenous design and development capabilities. We are excited to support promising startups in Semiconductors and utilize BITS Pilani’s capabilities in Semiconductor research and innovation for overall ecosystem development.” Prof. Sanket Goel, Dean (Research & Innovation) and Convenor, CREST, said, “The Semiconductor Accelerator, will bridge the critical gap between innovative ideas and market reality for early-stage startups in chip design. By leveraging resources, mentorship, and industry connections, this dynamic program will empower students and recent graduate entrepreneurs to thrive. By providing access to seed funding, state-of-the-art labs, and a collaborative learning environment, the Accelerator will foster the next generation of innovators, shaping the future of this transformative technology.” Interested startups can apply to the CREST accelerator program by logging on to the MSH’s website https://msh.meity.gov.in/ PIEDS PIED Society is a leading platform that helps entrepreneurs get access to resources that can help them come together and innovate the future of India. It is a Technology Business Incubator at BITS Pilani which was set up in 2004 with support from Department of Science and Technology, Government of India, and have incubated many campus and alumni driven startups. With a mission to support every innovative idea that can scale to be successful, PIED Society works with startups from all BITS campuses, its alumni, and beyond. Micron Micron is a world leader in innovative memory solutions that transform how the world uses information. For over 45 years, Micron has been instrumental to the world’s most significant technology advancements, delivering optimal memory and storage systems for a broad range of applications. CREST With a vision to make BITS Pilani as the leading academic institution for Semiconductor Education, Research and Innovation in India, Center for Research Excellence in Semiconductor Technologies (CREST) was launched in 2023. Since then, CREST is on the forefront of diversified activities, including professional development courses, hands-on training programs, minor-and-major degree programs, and organizing lectures. CREST houses labs in the entire value-chain of semiconductors, and supports faculty members, PhD scholars and interns to implement many innovative projects, and collaborated with various international and national academic institutes and industry. More details: https://crest.bits-pilani.ac.in/ . Apply Now

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > SAMSUNG "SOLVE FOR TOMORROW" Samsung "Solve for Tomorrow" Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology’s (MeitY’s) Startup Hub announced its partnership with Samsung India for the second season of its national education and innovation competition Samsung Solve for Tomorrow that aims to usher in a culture of innovative thinking and problem-solving amongst the country’s youth. The second season of Samsung Solve for Tomorrow was inaugurated by Shri. Alkesh Kumar Sharma, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology (MeitY) in the presence of Prof. Rangan Banerjee, Director, IIT Delhi, Mr. Jeet Vijay, CEO, MeitY Startup Hub and Mr. JongBum Park, President & CEO, Samsung Southwest Asia. Message by Honorable Secretary MeitY, Shri. Alkesh Kumar Sharma “The vision of the Government of India is to promote a vibrant ecosystem of innovation and entrepreneurship in the country, and youth are at the centre of this. They need all the support and mentoring that can help them turn their ideas into reality, creating a strong social impact along the way. The coming together of Samsung, IIT Delhi, MeitY Startup Hub and NITI Aayog AIM for the Solve for Tomorrow program is a very positive move that will work towards realizing our vision.”

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > MSH CORPORATE > GOOGLE APPSCALE ACADEMY 2023 IMPACT Google Appscale Academy 2023 Impact Google Appscale Academy the Class of 2023, was executed with 100 startups selected from over 950 applications, represents a diverse range of sectors, including education, health, social impact, gaming, agriculture, and more. These innovative startups have harnessed technology to address critical needs, making a tangible impact on society. Appscale Academy has been instrumental in training and mentoring early to mid-stage startups, empowering them to build high-quality apps for both local and global markets. A comprehensive Google Appscale Academy 2023 VC Handbook showcasing the success stories of the program. The VC handbook serves as a consolidated repository, featuring details of all the selected startups. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. Indian developers continue to push boundaries by building innovative solutions for many complex challenges. Be it apps supporting diverse communities or global games emerging from beyond metro cities, we’re seeing inspiring local developers across the country underscore India’s rise as a powerful app development hub. The program is outlined with a customized curriculum, including virtual instructor-led webinars, self-learning material, and mentorship sessions with leading local and global professionals from the industry. he curated, 6-month training program will identify, support and nurture promising Indian startups to supercharge their growth on Android. Google Appscale Academy 2023 Roadshows To build general awareness for the program amongst the broader ecosystem, especially in beyondmetro cities and supercharge their growth on Android, MeitY Startup Hub and Google organized 5 roadshows in 5 cities (Surat, Indore, Coimbatore, Jaipur, Gangtok) with the 5 incubation centres as implementation partners (AIC-RAISE - Coimbatore, Incub8 - Surat, Headstart, SGSITS-Shri Govindram Institute of Technology and Science - Indore, JECRC - Jaipur Engineering College and Research Centre, Atal Incubation Centre Sikkim Manipal University Technology Business Incubation Foundation (AIC-SMUTBI) - Gangtok). Google Appscale Academy 2023 Top 100 Reveal Event at India Habitat Centre Hon'ble Joint Secretary, MeitY, Shri Akash Tripathi unveiled the class of 2023 of Appscale Academy 2023. He added "India, currently holding the prestigious Presidency of G20 nations, has grown from being the tenth largest economy ten years ago to the fifth largest today. India has been the fastest growing major economy for the third year in a row—assured for further growth, driven strongly by, among other factors, a vigorous and diverse technology ecosystem. Google and MeitY's partnership through MSH have added a lot of value to the Indian startup ecosystem. My Best wishes to the Class of 2023" The chosen early to mid-stage startups will be guided by Appscale Academy to build high-quality apps for the world. The 100 startups were chosen from over 950 applications following an in-depth selection process that considered their creative ideas and innovation, product quality, product scalability, and talent diversity. 50% of the cohort representing major sectors like education, health, social, agriculture and more come from beyond metro cities including Varanasi, Ernakulam,Surat, Dehradun, Allahabad and more. Addressing the Top 100 startups selected for the program, CEO, MSH, Mr. Jeet Vijay added, "India offers rainbow of opportunities for the startups and entrepreneurs. India is showing way for the world with its dominance in digital public goods. India Stack, CoWin, Aadhar, UPI, Digilocker etc. are the biggest contributions of digital public goods that India can make to the world. More importantly this makes it possible for every society and economy to be more inclusive. It’s about tech that can be used for everyone in the world. It’s about a common man being able to use the power of technology and do something that is useful to them."

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 Shortlisted Startups XR Startup Program A program to discover, nurture, and accelerate XR technology startups across India. About the program XR Startup Program is a collaboration between Meta and MeitY Startup Hub (MSH). The program aims to accelerate India’s contribution towards building the foundations of the metaverse and nurturing the development of Extended Reality (XR) technologies in India. The metaverse is the next generation of the internet. It will be a set of digital spaces, including 3D experiences that are all interconnected so people can easily move between them and will let people do things they could not do in the physical world. While the defining quality of the metaverse will be a feeling of presence, the defining technologies of the metaverse will be Extended Reality (XR), including Augmented Reality (AR), Virtual Reality (VR) and Mixed Reality (MR). Click here to read more Accelerator About the Accelerator The Accelerator will nurture and foster 40 early-stage startups working with XR technologies through a well-designed 6-month program. Each startup will get access to a number of benefits, including a grant of ₹ 20,00,000, mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at relevant industry events. North zone - FITT South zone - FITT East zone - FITT West zone - FITT Grand Challenge About the Grand Challenge The Grand Challenge is aimed at supporting early-stage innovators to upscale from the R&D phase to developing workable prototypes and Minimum Viable Products (MVPs). It will include 4 sector-specific challenges to advance the XR technology ecosystem in India. A total of 80 shortlisted innovators will receive a grant of ₹ 50,000 and an opportunity to attend the bootcamp. Of the 80 shortlisted innovators, 16 innovators will be supported with grants worth ₹ 20,00,000 to help them develop MVP(s)/ prototypes. Benefits include mentorship by industry experts and researchers, access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, technology and infrastructure support, and opportunities to participate and present at industry events. North zone - FITT South zone - FITT East zone - FITT West zone - FITT Implementation partners Frequently Asked Questions How does one apply to the program? Accelerator – You can select any 1 of the implementation partners mentioned above and click on the relevant link to learn more and apply. Participants will be required to visit the facility/ campus of the partner; hence, it is best to choose the partner situated in or closer to your region. Grand Challenge – Depending on your focus area of innovation, you can select any 1 of the implementation partners mentioned above and click on the relevant link to learn more and apply. What is the selection process of the program? Each implementation partner will establish a 3-member panel to evaluate and select the applications. Applications will then be discussed with the steering committee established by Meta in collaboration with MSH. More details are available on the website of the relevant implementation partner. What are the program benefits? Under the Accelerator, 40 selected startups will receive the following benefits: Grant of ₹ 20,00,000 Mentorship by industry experts and researchers Access to a network of potential strategic partners and investors, along with technology and infrastructure support Opportunities to participate and present at industry events Whom should one contact in case of queries? The respective implementation partner can be contacted for queries on email: FITT: tidefitt@gmail.com CIE IIIT-H: incubation@iiith.org and cieoutreach@iiit.ac.in GUSEC: programs@gusec.edu.in SMU-TBI: contact@smutbi.com For more details, visit the website of the relevant implementation partner. What are the terms and conditions of the program? The program terms and conditions are as below: Accelerator: Refer here Grand Challenge: Refer here How many startups will be supported under the Accelerator? A total of 40 startups (10 by each implementation partner) will be supported and will receive program benefits as mentioned above. How many innovators will be supported under the Grand Challenge? A total of 80 (20 by each implementation partner) shortlisted innovators will receive the shortlist grant and be invited by the respective implementation partners to attend a 2-week bootcamp. Of these, 16 innovators (4 by each implementation partner) will be selected for support and program benefits. Is it possible to apply to more than 1 implementation partner? Applications should be made to only 1 implementation partner.

The program was initiated by the signature of the Indo-France Digital Roadmap by Honourable Prime Minister of India, Shri. Narendra Modi, and Honourable President of France, Emmanuel Macron in August 2019. IFTECH was sponsored by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), and La French Tech. It was managed and implemented by MeitY Startup Hub and LINK (formerly NUMA Bengaluru). It was supported by Software Technology Parks of India (STPI), The French Embassy in India and Business France. pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 As part of its mission to accelerate international market access and foster long-term global partnerships, MeitY Startup Hub led a high-impact delegation of Indian deep-tech startups to Brussels from May 26–29, 2025, under the MeitY Global Startup Bridge Program. This edition aimed to connect Indian startups with the European innovation ecosystem through cross-border collaboration, policy engagement, and knowledge exchange.The program focused on enabling market access, forging corporate and government partnerships, unlocking investment opportunities, facilitating technology exchange, and deepening ecosystem engagement. The Brussels edition reaffirmed MeitY Startup Hub’s vision of enabling Indian startups to scale globally through strategic partnerships, international exposure, and ecosystem-level collaboration. With the MeitY Global Startup Bridge initiative, MeitY Startup Hub positions itself as a strategic facilitator, bridging Indian deep-tech startups with the global market. The initiative addresses core startup needs such as international market access, investor and corporate connections, regulatory guidance, R&D collaboration, and ecosystem visibility. But beyond access and funding, the MeitY Startup Hub also opens doors to the right people, platforms, and partnerships, ensuring startups are not just supported, but strategically connected to scale globally. If you're a startup looking to grow beyond borders, not just through research or capital, but by plugging into the right global networks, this is your gateway. The signing of MoU between MeitY Startup Hub and hub.brussels at the Indian Embassy, by Dr. Paneerselvam Madanagopal , CEO, MeitY Startup Hub, and Mr. Bart Vandeputte , Trade and Invest Commissioner, hub.brussels, in the presence of Ambassador Shri Saurabh Kumar , underscoring a shared commitment to foster India–Belgium innovation partnerships. A key highlight was the signing of an MoU between Indian startup IG Drones and Belgian firm VoxelSensors , aimed at advancing bilateral collaboration in drone and sensor technologies. - Click on the tabs to know more: Why Brussels? Navigating Work Permits in Brussels Investment Landscape in Brussels R&D: The Incentive Guide Stay Corporate Connected

pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > Global Startup Bridge > VIVATECH 2023 VIVATECH 2023 In continuity to Indo-France relationship during 14-17 June, 2023, as part of bilateral trade initiative 62 Indian startups have showcased their participation in the event, these startup were supported under Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology and Department (MEITY) Startup Hub, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology and Department and the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry. These startup get global opportunities to pitch the investor, showcased their product and also had networking opportunity to grow their business. The Pavilion hosted Mr. Maurice levy, Chairman of the Supervisory Board, Publicis Groupe, and Ms. Valérie Pécresse, President of Region IDF at India Pavilion. Read more

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 PROGRAMS > Global Startup Bridge > VIVATECH VIVATECH 2022 VIVATECH is Europe’s biggest startup and tech event which accelerates innovation by connecting startups, tech leaders, major corporations, and investors responding to the world’s biggest challenges. From keynote speakers and exhibits to labs and live experiences, VIVATECH 2022 was a celebration of today’s innovative technologies that have the power to transform the future. India was the “Country of the Year” partner of VIVATECH 2022 in Paris. MeitY Startup Hub delegation participated in the 4-day event and the startups were showcased at the India’s pavilion. The pavilion highlighted the Indian startup ecosystem, partnership ecosystem, and provided the platform for startups to engage with investors, corporates, and other prospective partners.The conference was inaugurated by Shri. Ashwini Vaishnaw, Minister of Railways, Communication, Electronics and Information Technology.

pages.title: MeityStartupHub
pages.text: MeityStartupHub Skip to main content meity-sthub@gov.in Contact : +91-11-24301244 A + A - A A A PARTICIPATE MedTech OCP 4.0 MedTech is a Center of Entrepreneurship in the field of MediElectronics and Health Deadline: Mar 29, 2023 RESOURCES > STARTUP SCHOOL Empower Your Startup Journey with MeitY Startup School Welcome to the MeitY Startup School, your one-stop platform for founder education. Learn from industry experts through live and recorded masterclass sessions. Access curated videos on startup essentials, covering topics like funding and product development. Explore our knowledge base filled with articles, guides, and resources on legal, marketing, and growth strategies. Connect with fellow founders, share experiences, and gain insights from the community. Stay informed about upcoming webinars, workshops, and networking events to thrive in the startup ecosystem. Learn More Unlock Your Potential with Masterclass Sessions by Industry Experts Discover the secrets to success in the startup world with our Masterclass Sessions. Learn from industry experts as they share their knowledge and insights on various topics. Whether you're a budding entrepreneur or an experienced founder, these sessions will provide you with valuable information and guidance to accelerate your startup journey. Previous Next Filters Tags + Accounting Business Development EV Early Stage Education Entrepreneurship Finance Podcast Organization + Harward University NPTEL Showreel Y-Combinator Videos Certifications Articles EV,Business Development Unveiling the Truth Behind EVs Join us in this enlightening conversation with Sabir Bhatia as we delve into the world of electric vehicles (EVs) and dissect the realities behind the revolution. Education,Entrepreneurship,Podcast Entrepreneurship in Emerging Economies Join us for an insightful conversation with tech pioneer Sabeer Bhatia as we delve into the future of education. Entrepreneurship,Business Development Entrepreneurship in Emerging Economies Explore how entrepreneurship and innovation tackle complex social problems in emerging economies. Accounting,Finance Introduction to Managerial Accounting Managerial Accounting by Dr. Varadraj Bapat,Department of Management,IIT Bombay.For more details on NPTEL visit http://nptel.ac.in Early Stage,Entrepreneurship How to Start a Startup - A course Y Combinator taught at Stanford Over the years Y Combinator has hosted a number of talks at Stanford University as part of a series known as "How to Start a Startup." Here are some of those lectures, panels, and Q&A sessions. Early Stage,Entrepreneurship The biggest mistakes that first time founders make Videos and essays for founders just getting started Startup,Entrepreneurship Startup India Learning Program Startup India Learning Program is a free online Entrepreneurship program by Startup India, a GOI initiative. Invest India in collaboration with UpGrad has developed this program. The aim is to help entrepreneurs get their ideas and ventures to the next level through structured learning. The program covers lessons on key areas of starting up by 40+ top founders of India in an extensive 4-Week Program. No articles found. Informative Unlock Your Startup Potential with Our Knowledge Base Join the MeitY Startup School and gain access to expert knowledge and a supportive community. Valuable Resources Access a wealth of knowledge on legal, marketing, growth strategies, and more. Connect & Learn Join our community of founders to share experiences and learn from each other. No.MeitY /IPR/TIDE/16(1)/2017 
Government of India 
Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology 
Innovation and IPR Division 
Electronics Niketan, 6, CGO Complex 
 
New Delhi-110003 
Dated: 1st Febuary, 2019 
 
ADMINISTRATIVE APPROVAL 
 
Subject: Administrative Approval for the Implementation of the scheme entitled “Technology 
Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs (TIDE) 2.0” at 51 Institutes of Higher 
Learning and Premier R&D Organisations.  
 
 The undersigned is directed to convey t he Administrative Approval of the Competent 
Authority to the implementation of the above said scheme to be implemented by Innovation and 
IPR Division , MeitY  at 51 Institutes of higher learning and premier R&D Organisations  with a 
budgetary outlay of  Rs. 264.62 Crores  (Rupees Two Hundred Sixty Four Crores and Sixty Two 
Lakhs only) to be released as grant-in aid over a period of five years. The details of the Scheme and 
terms & Conditions governing release of grant -in-aid from MeitY are given  in the Annexure -I & 
Annexure II respectively.   
 
3. This issues with the approval of Minister of Electronics & IT  vide Diary No. 06 dated 
17.01.2019 and with the concurrence of AS&FA, MeitY vide OPA No. 212 dated 26.12.2018. 
 
    
(Jatinder Singh)

2. Office of the Director General of Audit, Post and Telecommunications, Shamnath Marg, 
Civil Lines, Near Old Secretariat, Delhi – 110054.

(Jatinder Singh)

Annexure II 
Terms & Conditions for Grant-in-Aid 
 
 
The grant is for the specific project as approved by Ministry of Electronics & Information Technology 
(MeitY) and shall be subject to the conditions listed below. The proposal originating industry and grantee 
institution shall give an undertaking that they agree to be governed by these conditions. 
 
1. The grant amount shall be i) spent for the project within the specified time; and ii) Any portion of the 
grant, which is not ultimately required for expenditure for the approved purposes, shall be duly 
surrendered to MeitY; 
 
2. The grantee institution shall maintain an audited record in the form of a register in the prescribed 
proforma for permanent, semi-permanent assets acquired solely or mainly out of MeitY grant; 
 
3. The assets referred to in (2) above will be property of MeitY and should not, without prior sanction 
of MeitY, be disposed off or encumbered or utilized for the purposes other than those for which the 
grant has been sanctioned; 
 
4. At the conclusion of the project, MeitY will be free to sell or otherwise dispose of the asse ts which 
are the property of MeitY and grantee institution shall render to MeitY the necessary support for 
facilitating the sale of these assets; 
 
5. The grantee institution shall send to the MeitY at the end of each financial year as well as at the time 
of seeking further installments of the grant a list of assets referred to in (2) above; 
 
6. Should at any time grantee institution cease to exist, such assets etc., shall revert to MeitY; 
 
7. The grantee institution shall render progress-cum-achievement reports at interval of not exceeding six 
months on the progress made on all aspects of the project including expenditure incurred on various 
approved items during the period; 
 
8. The grantee institution shall render an audited statement of accounts to MeitY; 
 
9. The audited  statement of accounts relating to grants given during financial year together with the 
comments of the auditor regarding the observance of the conditions governing the grant should be 
forwarded to the MeitY within six months following the end of the relevant financial year; 
 
10. The utilization of grant for the intended purposes will be looked into by the Auditor of grantee 
institution according to the directives issued by the Government of India at the instance of the 
Comptroller and Auditor General and the specific mention about it will be made in the audit report; 
 
11. MeitY or its nominee(s) will have the right of access to the books and accounts of the grantee 
institution for which a reasonable prior notice would be given; 
 
 
 
12. The grantee institution should mai ntain separate audited account for the project. If it is found 
expedient to keep a part or whole of the grant in a bank account earning interest, all the interest or 
other earnings against GIA should be mandatorily remitted to the Consolidated Fund of Indi a 
immediately after  finalization of accounts. Such advances shall not be allowed to be adjusted against 
further releases in pursurance of GFR provisions. 
 
13. Institute may retain the sale proceeds of prototypes, etc fabricated as a result of the development of 
the project arising directly from funds granted by the Ministry. The Institute may use funds thus 
generated for furtherance of project objectives; 
 
14. The Intellectual property and the rights associated with it shall be agreed between the parti cipating 
organizations before the start of the project. The Industry/ Industry Consortium/ Institution(s) will 
make all efforts to protect intellectual property generated out of the project. The 
institution(s)/industry would submit the periodic report to M eitY for a period of minimum 5years on 
the status of IPRs created/commercialization under the project. (5 years is the expected life time of 
product/technology). Furthermore, IPR must also reside in India so that India has access and 
complete control to these rights in times of emergency to protect our national interest; 
 
15. Application by grantee institution for any other financial assistance or receipt of grant/loan from any 
other Agency/Ministry/Department for this project should have the prior approval of Ministry of 
Electronics & Information Technology; 
 
16. The Grantee institution(s) is not allowed to entrust the implementation of this project for which grant-
in-aid is received to another institution and to divert the grant -in-aid received from Ministry of 
Electronics & Information Technology as assistance to the later institution; 
 
17. MeitY shall appoint a Project Review and Steering Group (PRSG) comprising of representatives 
from MeitY and other experts. PRSG will periodically monitor the project in all respect s including 
technical and financial; 
 
18. In case of any dispute on any matter, related to the project during the course of its implementation, 
the decision of the Secretary, MeitY, shall be final and binding on the proposal originating industry/ 
industry consortium and grantee institute.

INCUBATION FUND MANAGEMENT 
Investment Playbook  
for  
Startup Incubators 
 
 
AUTHORS 
Dr. Panneerselvam Madangopal 
Poyni Bhatt 
Prof. Rohan Chinchwadkar 
 
 
 
 
December 2025 
 
 
 
 
 

   
2 
 
 
 
 
About The Authors:
Dr. Panneersel vam Madangopal  is CEO of MeitY 
Startup Hub and Director – Digital India Program of 
Ministry of Electronic and Information Technology, 
Government of India. His core expertise is in innovation 
management, business strategy and ecosystem 
building. He  is also an educator in the domain of 
innovation and entrepreneurship. 
Poyni Bhatt is a founding team member and former 
CEO of SINE, the technology business incubator at IIT 
Bombay. She has mentored more than 250 startups 
within and outside SINE. She has also contributed to 
various central and state government departments in 
shaping their entrepreneurship programs. She is an 
Advisor- Knowledge and Resource Centre of  Indian 
STEP & Business Incubator Association. A qualified 
legal and compliance professional, she  has an 
experience of nearly three decades in industry and 
academia. 
Prof. Rohan Chinchwadkar  is an Assistant Professor of 
Finance at the Shailesh J. Mehta School of 
Management, an Associate Faculty at the Desai Sethi 
School of Entrepreneurship and a Me mber of the 
Institute Innovation Council at IIT Bombay. He has 
worked closely with several units of the central 
government like the Ministry of Education and NITI 
Aayog (Atal Innovation Mission). He is a Research 
Advisor for ISBA’s Knowledge and Resource Centre. He 
holds a PhD from IIM Calcutta and has worked as a 
management consultant with McKinsey & Company. He 
has co -edited the book "Shifting Orbits: Decoding the 
Trajectory of the Indian Startup Ecosystem".  
*Authors’ views are personal.
